So the crowd waited. Seconds ticked by. Each heartbeat was an eternity. The screams turned into whispers, then hushes, then breathless stillness.

No one dared blink.

Then the mist began to part. Slowly, softly, like curtains unveiling the final act of a legendary play.

Through it, the answer would be revealed.

The mist finally thinned.

What had been a battlefield moments ago was now a canvas of frost and silence, painted by two young prodigies who had poured everything into a single clash.

The first figure to emerge from the mist was Mirabella. Her steps were shaky, her shoulders slumped. Blood still ran from her nose, her chitin armor cracked in multiple places.

Her breath came in ragged, uneven bursts. But her gaze burned with quiet triumph.

She had won.

The crowd didn’t erupt right away. They simply gasped. Because the next moment, Ren’s body came into view, partially buried in broken frost at the arena’s edge.

His chest rose and fell slowly. His kusarigama lay beside his hand, its blade cracked and chain covered in frost.

He was conscious. Barely. But he didn’t rise because he knew.

"Tch." He winced, letting out a dry chuckle that turned into a cough. "Guess that really was the second layer..."

Mirabella stepped forward, ignoring the chanting crowd now rising like a wave behind her.

"I thought I had you," Ren muttered, eyes still closed. "Even weaved a Mind attack for the first time... what a waste."

A shadow fell over him. He opened his eyes to find Mirabella standing over him, bloodied, victorious, and strangely solemn.

"You didn’t waste it," she said softly.

He blinked.

"You forced me to use everything," she continued. "Not just my runes or the second layer. But my entire being. My reflexes, my instincts, my will."

She crouched beside him, one hand gripping her side, wincing from pain.

"I’ve never gone that far before. Not even in my final trials back home."

Ren stared at her. The corners of his lips twitched.

"You’re saying I should be proud?"

"I’m saying you earned my respect."

She meant it. Her voice was steady, but her eyes betrayed something deeper. Something complicated.

That trace of disbelief, that flicker of awe. That quiet, dangerous tremble in her heart that made her breath quicken.

He reminded her so much of her father. Not in power or presence. But in how he handled loss.

Ren slowly sat up, ignoring the ache in his spine. He looked up at her with calm, intelligent eyes. Calm despite the blood on his lips, despite the pain coursing through every nerve.

He gave a half-smile.

"Guess this means I’m joining your guild, huh?"

Mirabella nodded, but her heart skipped again at how easily he accepted it. No bitterness. No begging. Just poise.

"You’ll be a most valuable addition," she said, standing fully again.

Ren stood next, swaying for a moment but catching himself.

"Damn right I will," he said, voice low but confident.

Even if he lost, he was proud of how far he’d come. No rich family, no special mentor, no secret weapons handed to him. And yet he came this far!

He turned toward the arena’s exit, hair fluttering gently.

"I’ll catch up soon, Ice Queen."

And with that, he walked away. The crowd parted for him like waves before a storm. Some shouted, some blushed, others clapped.

But Ren didn’t hear any of it. His fists were clenched tight. Not from shame, but from anger and resolve.

’Next time, I win.’

But for now, he had lost. And he had taken that loss like a king.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 56: An Enticing Offer!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The walk back to the dormitories was exactly what Ren needed. Time to think. Time to process what had just happened and what it meant for his future as the only male weaver in recorded history.

He pulled out a small notebook from his cloak pocket, already mentally cataloging his weaknesses from the fight. His handwriting was steady despite his injuries as he walked.

’Stamina management - need better conditioning. Mind Path weaving - requires more practice, backlash too severe. Barrier techniques - study reinforcement patterns. Chain weapon mastery - explore weighted variations.’

The Mind Blast had been promising, but the toll it took on his mental sea was unsustainable. He’d need to find training methods that didn’t leave him vulnerable after a single use.

Still, he was sure that had the chain Mirabella wire around her neck not been an enchanted item with the ability to block mental attacks, he would have taken her by surprise and won.

Even the necklace had cracked from sustaining that attack and she had also spit blood!

It would have been his win for sure! But there was no point lamenting.

The problem was finding instructors willing to teach him. He couldn’t learn everything on his own. He needed personal and expert guidance.

But most female weavers still viewed him with suspicion or outright hostility so he would most likely have to achieve this benefit with money.

But overall, the current issue wasn’t his combat abilities. It was the political minefield he’d just stepped into as history’s greatest anomaly.

Mirabella’s offer hadn’t been spontaneous kindness. The Frostweave family was one of the most influential weaving dynasties in this Sovereignty.

Her decision to recruit the first male weaver had definitely been discussed, analyzed, approved by people much more powerful than either of them.

The question was why. What did they want from him?

Ren paused under a lamppost, tapping his pen against the notebook. As the sole male weaver, he represented either an unprecedented opportunity or an existential threat to the established order.

The Frostweave family could be trying to control him before other factions made their moves. Or they might want to study him, figure out what made him different from every other man who’d tried and failed to weave.

Worse, they might see him as a breeding asset. The thought made his jaw clench. If his abilities could be passed down, every major family would want to secure that bloodline for themselves.

He needed allies outside the Frostborne influence. Information networks. Backup plans. Most importantly, he needed to prove his value lay in more than just his genetic uniqueness.

"Ren!"

The voice cut through his strategizing. He looked up to see Lia jogging toward him, her black hair catching the lamplight as she moved.

Her green eyes were bright with excitement, completely at odds with his calculating mood.

"That was incredible!" she said, slightly breathless as she caught up to him.

"The way you adapted during the fight, revealing a second affinity in the middle of combat against someone using second-layer weaves. You just proved that male weavers aren’t just possible, they can be exceptional."

Ren closed his notebook and slipped it back into his cloak. "You don’t need to console me, Lia. I still lost."

"Console you?" She looked genuinely confused. "I’m not trying to console you. I’m telling you that you just changed everything. Do you understand what you did in there? You didn’t just fight Mirabella, you fought centuries of assumption about what men can and cannot do! You were awesome to be able to do this in less than a month!"

He studied her face. Lia had been one of the few people who’d treated him ’normally’ from the beginning, neither fawning over his uniqueness nor dismissing him as a freak. If she said he’d accomplished something significant, she meant it.

"Every weaver in that arena saw a man not just survive against elite-level techniques, but inspire under pressure," she continued as they walked. "You unleashed a Mind weave which is one of the hardest weaves for low ranked weavers in the middle of actual combat. That’s not just rare, Ren. That’s legendary."

"And still lost," Ren pointed out.

"So what?" Lia’s voice carried a hint of frustration. "You think the first female weavers won every battle when they were establishing themselves? Progress isn’t measured in single victories. It’s measured in proving what’s possible."

They walked in comfortable silence for a moment. Ren found himself appreciating her perspective. It was easy to get lost in the disappointment of defeat, but she was right about the broader implications.

Every demonstration of his abilities normalized the idea of male weavers.

"Besides," Lia said, her voice softer now, "the way you handled losing in front of everyone was... well, it was dignified. No excuses, no blame. Just acceptance and determination to improve. You represented yourself, and by extension all future male weavers, with honor."

Something in her tone made him glance at her. There was a warmth in her expression that went beyond professional respect.

An intensity that had nothing to do with his historical significance and everything to do with him as a person.

"You know," she said, looking straight ahead, "being the first of anything is a lonely burden. If there’s anything I can do to help you carry that weight, or if you need anything to remind you that you’re not just a symbol but a person too... I mean, anything at all..."

The offer hung in the air between them. Ren felt a shift in the atmosphere, a charge that had nothing to do with weaving energy. Lia’s cheeks had the faintest hint of color, and she was being unusually careful with her words.

His strategic mind immediately recognized the opportunity. The attraction that had been building between them for weeks was finally surfacing.

But more than that, she was offering something precious: the chance to be seen as just Ren, not the anomalous male weaver.

He could let the moment pass, maintain their current dynamic. Or he could push forward and claim something for himself, not for history or politics, but for his own desires.

Ren stopped walking. Lia took another step before realizing he’d halted, then turned to face him. The lamplight cast shadows across her features, making her green eyes seem to glow.

He closed the distance between them until they were barely a foot apart.

"Anything?" he asked, his voice low and deliberate.

The word seemed to electrify the space around them. Lia’s breath caught, her eyes widening slightly as the full weight of what she’d offered settled between them.

The sexual tension that had been simmering for weeks suddenly surged to the surface, undeniable and intense.

....

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 57: Wanna Shower Together?
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The word hung in the air between them like a promise. Lia’s cheeks flushed deeper, and for a moment, neither of them moved.

The lamplight seemed to dim everything else around them, creating a private world where only they existed.

"Anything," she whispered back, her voice barely audible but carrying absolute certainty.

Ren reached up slowly, giving her every chance to pull away. His fingers traced along her jawline, gentle but deliberate.

Her skin was warm despite the cold air, and he could feel the slight tremor that ran through her at his touch.

"You’re sure?" he asked, his thumb brushing across her cheek.

Instead of answering with words, Lia rose up on her tiptoes, closing the remaining distance between them.

Their lips met in a kiss that was soft at first, tentative, almost questioning. Then her hands found the front of his shirt, pulling him closer, and the kiss deepened.

The kiss wasn’t rushed. It wasn’t wild or desperate. It was slow, deliberate, like two pieces of a puzzle finally deciding to fit.

Lia’s lips brushed his with the gentleness of a prayer, and Ren, despite all his sharpness, all his calculations, melted into it.

His hands moved instinctively—one coming to coil around her neck, thumb grazing her jaw, the other wrapping around her waist and pulling her closer.

She tasted like tea and something sweet beneath it, like a memory of something warm on a cold day.

And when she breathed his name against his lips, everything else—the arena, the loss, the future—fell away.

When they finally broke apart, both were breathing a little harder. Lia’s eyes sparkled with mischief and something deeper.

"For someone who just got thoroughly beaten in combat," she said, her voice playful, "you certainly know how to make a recovery."

Ren chuckled, his forehead resting against hers. "Maybe I was saving my best techniques for after the fight."

"Oh really?" Lia’s eyebrows arched in amusement. "And what other techniques are you hiding?"

"Wouldn’t you like to know?" He grinned, taking her hand as they started walking again toward the dormitories. "Though I should warn you, some of them require more... private training grounds."

"Is that so?" She squeezed his hand, her thumb tracing circles on his palm. "Lucky for you, I’m an excellent training partner. Very dedicated to... thorough practice sessions."

The flirtation continued as they walked, their voices low and warm in the quiet pathways.

By the time they reached the dormitory building, the tension between them had transformed from desperate attraction to something comfortable and sweet, like they’d been doing this dance for years instead of minutes.

At the door leading to their room, Ren paused. "My bed?" he asked simply.

Lia nodded, her expression shifting to something more serious but no less warm. "I’d like that."

Ren held himself back from fist bumping the skies and screaming at the top of his voice.

Patience, boy!

His bed was small but organized, with two pillows and fluffy sheets. It was sparse but somehow welcoming, very much like Ren himself.

Lia settled onto his bed without invitation, drawing her legs up and watching as he moved around the small space. There was something natural about her presence there, like she belonged.

"Come here," she said softly, patting the space beside her.

Ren joined her, and they sat close together, her shoulder pressed against his. For a few minutes, they just existed in comfortable silence, processing everything that had happened.

"Lia," Ren said finally, his voice gentle but serious. "Can I ask you something?"

She turned to face him, sensing the shift in his tone. "Of course."

"During the curse siege... something happened to you. You changed." His silver eyes searched her face.

"The way you fought, especially when you... when you destroyed that shapeshifting curse. There was so much anger, so much vengeance. I haven’t been able to get it out of my mind."

Lia’s expression grew distant, and she was quiet for a long moment. When she spoke, her voice was barely above a whisper.

"I was an orphan," she began. "Living on the streets, scavenging, stealing when I had to. I thought that was all there was for me."

She paused, gathering herself. "Then one day, this old man found me. He was kind, gentle. He took me to the surface, gave me a home, put me in school. His name was Henrik."

Ren remained silent, sensing she needed to tell this story in her own way.

"He was barely a carver, not much power, and he was already old when he found me. But he taught me everything - how to read, how to be strong, how to believe in myself. He was... he was the only family I ever had."

Her voice began to shake slightly. "One day I came home from school, calling his name like I always did. He was sitting in his chair in the living room, and I was so happy to see him. I ran over and started telling him about my day, about the physical exercises we’d practiced."

Lia’s hands clenched into fists in her lap. "But something was wrong. He wasn’t responding the way he should. His eyes were... empty. Glassy. And there was this smell, this awful sweet smell of decay."

Ren felt his stomach tighten as he realized where this was going.

"I asked him our code phrase," she continued, her voice breaking slightly. "Something we’d always done, just a silly thing between us.

"But he couldn’t answer. Because he wasn’t Henrik anymore. He’d been dead for hours, maybe all day, and that thing was just... wearing him. Puppeting his body."

Ren listened to Lia’s voice as if each word she spoke was a needle threading through his chest. The room felt quieter with every sentence; quieter and heavier.

But even as she told her story, even as his chest tightened at the horror of it, another part of his mind was working at full capacity.

’Okay. This is a trauma reveal. High-stakes emotional vulnerability. Respond with empathy, not logic. No jokes. Definitely no jokes. Hold hand? Wait—no. Too soon. Or maybe not soon enough?’

He shifted slightly, trying to gauge the optimal moment for physical contact based on the cadence of her voice and the tremble in her fingers.

When she mentioned the curse puppeting Henrik’s body, something cold stabbed into Ren’s gut.

Tears began to form in her eyes, but her voice grew harder. "Although the curse was probably planning to eat me too, for some reason, it didn’t do anything to me.

"It seemed to be waiting, patient, using his body to lure me close. When I figured it out and tried to run, it dropped the pretense.

"Started chasing me with his face, his voice, telling me how much it was going to enjoy consuming me the way it had consumed him."

"Lia..." Ren reached for her hand.

’That’s it. Maintain eye contact. Nod occasionally. Validate. Empathize. Look invested, not pitiful.’

’You’re not a therapist, you’re... you’re a future lifelong emotional support partner. Crap, that sounded way too intense. Just breathe. She’s not reading your thoughts!’

But Lia was speaking again, and the images she painted were brutal.

"I almost didn’t make it," she whispered. "It caught me right at the door. But then Miranda Frostweave appeared - Mirabella’s older sister. She killed it easily, saved me. But I had to see... I had to see what was left of Henrik after it was done feeding."

Ren swallowed thickly, already cataloging every word. Every detail. Because even though he wanted to comfort her now, he also wanted to understand her better, deeply, truly.

He wanted to memorize the architecture of her pain so that he would never, ever, trample over it unknowingly.

A few tears finally spilled over, tracking down her cheeks. "So when I saw that shapeshifter during the siege, pretending to be someone else, using someone’s appearance to hurt people... all I could think about was Henrik’s face. His kind, gentle face being used by something evil."

Ren pulled her close, wrapping his arms around her as she trembled slightly. "I’m so sorry," he murmured against her hair. "I’m so sorry you had to go through that."

"I beat it to death," she said, her voice muffled against his chest. "I kept hitting it even after it stopped moving because I wanted to make sure it could never use anyone’s face again."

He expected her to collapse, but she didn’t. Her voice, muffled and fierce against his chest, was still that same flame.

"I beat it to death," she said. "I kept hitting it even after it stopped moving because I wanted to make sure it could never use anyone’s face again."

Ren tilted her chin up gently. His hand cupped her cheek.

’Now. Compliment. Ground her strength in something meaningful. Relate it to Henrik. Acknowledge her pain but remind her she’s more than it. That should do the trick... probably.’

"He would be proud of you," Ren said softly. "Henrik. He’d be proud of the woman you became, of how you protected other people from what happened to both of you."

Tears welled again, but she smiled. And that smile hit Ren harder than any rune, any attack. Because it was a smile through pain. A smile earned. A smile entrusted.

"You think so?"

"I know so."

This time when he kissed her, it wasn’t careful or calculated. It was slow and sure, but unfiltered.

A kiss not born from strategy, but from understanding. It was a silent contract. That she was seen. That her pain mattered. That her scars didn’t make her less, but more. It tasted of salt, but felt like warmth.

His mind, always so busy, went almost silent during that kiss.

’...Okay. Emotional bond has reached an estimated 67.4% increase. Affection reciprocity confirmed. Oxytocin levels likely elevated. Damn it. Stop calculating. Just...’

When they broke apart, Lia nestled against his shoulder, her breathing softening. They sat there for long, quiet minutes. No more planning, no more strategy.

Eventually, Ren stirred and broke the silence with a mischievous glint in his eyes. But in truth, it was just a smokescreen, because he still couldn’t calm his racing heart.

"I should probably shower," he said, running a hand through his hair. "I’m still covered in arena dust and frost."

He stood, paused, then glanced down at her—expression one part daring, one part amused, and another part internally screaming into a pillow.

"Wanna shower together?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 58: Sensual Shower. (1) (R18)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ren stood calmly by the edge of his dorm bed. His silver hair clung to his forehead, no longer damp with the sweat of battle.

His clothes were torn and stained with the crimson of his own blood and that of his opponent.

Lia’s gaze lingered on his bruised form, her emerald eyes filled with concern and embarrassment.

Her cheeks were colored a rosy pink, making her look even more gorgeous.

Ren had never seen anyone as adorable as she was at that moment, especially when she leaned in closer, her voluptuous body pressing against him.

With a smirk that hinted at the mischief brewing in her mind, she playfully poked his chest. "You know, for someone who just got their butt kicked, you’re pretty confident right now."

Her words danced with a teasing lilt, and she couldn’t help but admire the way the muscles beneath his torn shirt rippled in response to her touch.

Ren’s eyes met hers, a spark of amusement flickering in his gaze. He reached up and brushed a stray lock of hair from her cheek, his voice steady and unbothered.

The Amethyst Corps wasn’t actually the real power behind everything, because the true control of the sovereignty was held by three major factions that were constantly playing political games with each other like they were in some kind of never-ending championship tournament where the prize was control over millions of people’s lives.

These three factions were made up of seven incredibly powerful families that had been accumulating wealth, influence, and magical power for hundreds of years and were as terrifying as they got.

They were so rich and connected that they could probably buy entire cities just for fun, or make people disappear with nothing more than a casual conversation over afternoon tea.

Even if said person was inside the Number One academy of the surface.

No place was too secure to breach for them. It would merely take more effort.

The first faction was called the Neutral Faction, and despite their boring name that made them sound like they just wanted everyone to get along and share their toys nicely, they were actually incredibly smart and strategic.

Not only were they highly favored by the masses, they were playing both sides of every conflict between the other two factions while secretly building their own power base like master chess players who were thinking fifteen moves ahead while everyone else was still trying to figure out which piece was the horsey.

The Neutral Faction was composed of only two families, but these two families were so influential and wealthy that they could probably influence major decisions just by raising their eyebrows in disapproval at fancy dinner parties.

Then there was the Traditionalist Faction, which was made up of families who believed that the old ways of doing things were the best ways, and they wanted to keep everything exactly the same as it had been for centuries.

This was probably because change scared them. The old ways were well known, trusted and had no surprises. And also very, very brutal when the case arises.

Which was exactly how they wanted it.

The Traditionalists controlled a lot of the agricultural areas and had deep connections with religious organizations, which meant they had both the food supply and spiritual influence needed to keep large portions of the population on their side, and they weren’t afraid to use both of those advantages whenever they needed to pressure the other factions into agreeing with their plans.

The third and final faction was the Progressive Faction, and these were the families who wanted to change everything, modernize the sovereignty, adopt new technologies, and basically turn their entire world upside down and build it back up from the scratch under a new order.

They were as crazy as crazy fanatics could get and operated more like terrorists than the noble families they were.

The Progressives controlled most of the major cities, the trade routes, and the magical research institutions, which gave them access to cutting-edge innovations and the kind of wealth that came from being the first to market with revolutionary new ideas.

Still, their constant push for change also made them a lot of enemies among people who preferred their lives to be predictable and stable.

And right in the middle of all this political chaos and family drama that was more intense than any soap opera ever created, Mirabella’s Frostweave family was one of the two powerful families that made up the Neutral Faction.

This meant that Ren was now technically connected to one of the most influential political groups in the entire sovereignty, and he had absolutely no idea what he was supposed to do with that information except maybe panic quietly while trying to figure out how not to accidentally start a war by saying the wrong thing at the wrong time to the wrong person.

Ren looked around at all the memory orbs surrounding him like glowing evidence of just how complicated his life had become, and couldn’t help but laugh at the absurdity of it all.

Just a few weeks ago his biggest worry had been whether he would have enough money to buy the items he needed to grow stronger, and now he was sitting in a magical library trying to understand political schemes that could affect the lives of millions of people.

All he knew was this: the plot was already beginning to thicken. He had to remain alert to avoid falling into any scheme or conspiracy against his interests.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 62: Hidden Arena.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ren was still sitting on the comfortable couch, organizing his thoughts about the complex political web he had just learned about.

His mind was working through the connections between the three factions when a soft chime echoed from his academy badge.

The small silver emblem pinned to his chest began glowing with a gentle blue light.

He lifted the badge closer to read the message that appeared in floating letters above it. All prospectives were to report immediately to the Edge of the Block.

They were specifically instructed to gather at the white wall section and wait for further instructions.

Ren raised an eyebrow as he read the message twice to make sure he understood correctly.

This was unusual timing for any kind of announcement or gathering. Most academic activities were scheduled well in advance.

His first thought was whether this might be another special event like the curse siege from before.

That had been an unexpected test that caught everyone off guard. Perhaps the instructors had planned another surprise challenge to evaluate their progress and abilities.

But something felt different about this summons. The location was odd, and the timing seemed almost random. Ren had never heard of any activities taking place at the Edge of the Block before.

He carefully placed the dimmed memory orb back in its designated spot among the others. Rising from the couch, he stretched his arms and decided to head to the specified location.

Whatever this was about, he would find out soon enough.

As he approached the Edge of the Block, he could already hear the buzz of excited voices ahead.

The sound grew louder with each step, suggesting that quite a few prospectives had already gathered at the meeting point.

When Ren rounded the final corner, he saw exactly what he had expected. A large crowd of girls had already formed near the white wall section.

They were clustered in small groups, chatting animatedly about the mysterious summons they had all received.

Some were speculating about possible training exercises. Others wondered if this might be an announcement about their advancement to the next stage and actually becoming true students.

The energy in the group was a mix of excitement and nervous anticipation.

Ren calmly walked to one side of the gathering and stood with his hands casually tucked into his pockets.

He maintained his usual composed demeanor while observing the crowd and waiting to see what would happen next.

His silver eyes scanned the faces around him, noting the various expressions of curiosity and concern.

Better to stay calm and observe until more information became available.

The white wall they were gathered near stretched high above them, its surface smooth and seemingly solid.

Ren had walked past this section many times before but had never paid much attention to it. Now he found himself studying its appearance more carefully.

Suddenly, he felt small hands cover his eyes from behind. The touch was gentle but playful, and he immediately recognized the size and warmth of the hands.

Only one person would approach him this way.

"Lia?" he said without hesitation, his voice carrying its usual calm confidence.

The hands immediately pulled away, and he heard Lia’s delighted squeal behind him. She bounced around to face him, her eyes wide with amazement and her expression full of joy.

"How did you know it was me so easily?!" she exclaimed, practically vibrating with excitement at his quick recognition.

Ren turned to face her fully, a small smile playing at the corners of his mouth. "Your hands are softer than everyone else’s here," he said smoothly.

"And you’re the only person who would try to surprise me like that."

Lia’s cheeks turned slightly pink at his observation, but her smile only grew brighter. She seemed pleased that he had noticed such a detail about her.

Her energy was infectious, and several nearby girls glanced over at their interaction with curious expressions.

As Ren’s gaze moved past Lia, he noticed Mirabella standing quietly beside her.

She was observing the exchange with her usual composed expression, though there was something different about her demeanor today.

Ren nodded toward her with his characteristic expressionless face. It was a simple acknowledgment, neither friendly nor unfriendly, just neutral recognition of her presence.

Mirabella nodded back, but unlike his neutral expression, she wore a small mischievous smile.

There was even a faint blush coloring her cheeks, which was unusual for someone normally so controlled and confident.

This was the first time they had encountered each other since their battle where Ren had lost. The memory of that defeat lingered between them, creating a subtle tension that neither acknowledged directly.

The situation felt slightly awkward, but Ren chose to ignore the uncomfortable undercurrents.

Instead of dwelling on past events, Ren decided to focus on the current situation. He turned his attention back to Lia, who was still beaming at him with obvious happiness.

"What do you think this is all about?" he asked her, gesturing toward the crowd of gathered prospectives and the mysterious white wall.

Lia’s expression shifted to one of confusion as she shrugged her shoulders. "I have absolutely no idea," she admitted honestly. "The message was so vague that it could mean anything."

She looked around at the other prospectives, noting their similar expressions of puzzlement. "Everyone seems just as confused as we are," she added. "Nobody knows why we’re here or what we’re supposed to be waiting for."

Ren nodded and then looked toward Mirabella, curious about her thoughts on the situation. She had proven to be analytical and logical in her thinking, often seeing patterns that others missed.

Mirabella noticed his questioning gaze and spoke up calmly. "Given the unusual location and the sudden nature of the summons, this is likely something significant," she began, her voice thoughtful and measured.

"The Edge of the Block is not typically used for routine training or announcements," she continued.

"The fact that we’re specifically gathered at the white wall suggests there might be something special about this particular section."

Ren found himself agreeing with her reasoning. "That makes sense," he said. "The timing also seems deliberate rather than random."

Mirabella nodded and added, "If I had to guess, we’re about to discover something that has been hidden or kept secret until now. The instructors wouldn’t gather us here without a specific purpose."

Her logical deductions aligned with Ren’s own thoughts about the situation. Whatever was about to happen, it was clearly planned and intentional rather than spontaneous.

As if responding to their speculation, a deep rumbling sound suddenly echoed through the area.

The ground beneath their feet vibrated slightly, and the crowd of prospectives fell silent as everyone looked around nervously.

The rumbling grew stronger, and then something extraordinary happened.

A large portion of the tall white wall began to move, splitting apart with mechanical precision to reveal what lay beyond.

Hidden behind the wall was a doorway unlike anything any of them had seen before. It was massive, easily tall enough for three people to walk through side by side, and it glowed with a soft inner light that seemed to beckon them forward.

The prospectives stared in amazement at this hidden entrance that had been concealed right under their noses.

Excited murmurs rippled through the crowd as everyone tried to process what they were seeing.

"Did anyone know this was here?" someone whispered, but the question was met with shaking heads and expressions of wonder.

The discovery of this secret passage explained why they had been summoned to this specific location. Whatever lay beyond that doorway was clearly important enough to warrant such secrecy and dramatic revelation.

Without waiting for official instructions, the prospectives began moving toward the entrance.

Their curiosity had overcome any hesitation, and they trooped forward eagerly to see what secrets lay beyond the hidden door.

As the crowd of prospectives passed through the doorway, they found themselves in a space that defied all expectations. The hidden area opened into an arena so vast and magnificent that it took everyone’s breath away.

The arena was ridiculously grand in its design and scope. Its architecture combined sleek modern elements with uniquely ancient features, creating an atmosphere that was both intimidating and awe-inspiring.

The main floor stretched out before them like a massive stage, easily large enough to accommodate hundreds of people in combat or ceremony.

Around this central space, ten different smaller floors were arranged in tiers, each one designed for specific purposes that weren’t immediately clear.

Ren estimated that the entire arena could easily fit ten thousand people without feeling crowded.

The scale was beyond anything he had imagined could exist within the Side Block without being noticed. The ceiling soared high above them, supported by massive pillars that seemed to reach toward the sky.

Ancient symbols and modern magical arrays were carved into the walls and floors, glowing with subtle energy that made the entire space feel alive.

The combination of old and new created an atmosphere of timeless power and authority.

The design was both beautiful and functional, clearly built for important events that required both grandeur and practicality. Every detail seemed carefully planned to create maximum impact on anyone who entered.

As the Block B prospectives stood in amazement at the arena’s magnificence, something else caught their attention.

Two other massive doorways, identical to the one they had used, were also opening on opposite sides of the arena.

From these additional entrances, hundreds of other girls began streaming into the space.

Their clothing and badges identified them clearly as prospectives from the other blocks within the side block.

It didn’t take long for the Block B prospectives to realize what they were seeing. These were the prospectives from Block A and Block C, all summoned to this secret arena at the same time for the same mysterious purpose.

The three groups of prospectives now found themselves sharing this magnificent space, each group having discovered its existence simultaneously.

The arena suddenly felt much more significant and important than any of them had initially realized.

This was clearly not just a routine gathering or training exercise. The fact that all three blocks had been summoned together suggested something major was about to take place.

The atmosphere in the arena began to shift as the three groups became aware of each other’s presence.

What had started as excitement and wonder began to transform into something more complex and charged.

Almost immediately, and for no particularly logical reason, the atmosphere in the arena grew tense. The three groups of prospectives began regarding each other with obvious hostility and suspicion.

Block A prospectives clustered together on one side of the arena, their expressions wary as they observed the other two groups.

Block C prospectives gathered on the opposite side, their body language defensive and challenging.

Block B prospectives, including Mirabella and Lia, positioned themselves between the other two groups but maintained similar attitudes of caution and competitiveness.

The friendly excitement from moments before had completely disappeared.

Even Mirabella, usually so composed and logical, showed signs of this sudden group hostility.

Her expression had hardened, and she watched the other blocks with calculating eyes that suggested she was already assessing potential threats.

The tension in the air was thick enough to cut with a knife. Each group seemed to view the others as competitors or enemies rather than fellow prospectives working toward the same goals.

In the midst of all this rising hostility and group loyalty, only one person remained completely unaffected by the tribal tensions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 63: Inter-Block Tournament!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The tension between the three blocks continued to simmer in the magnificent arena.

Each group maintained their defensive positions while eyeing the others with barely concealed hostility.

The atmosphere was thick with competitive energy that seemed ready to explode at any moment.

Suddenly, the sound of measured footsteps echoed through the vast space. Multiple figures emerged from a previously hidden entrance high above the arena floor.

They descended a grand staircase that materialized from the wall itself, their movements coordinated and purposeful.

The approaching figures were clearly instructors from the academy. Their uniforms were more elaborate yet elegant, featuring intricate designs that spoke of high rank and authority.

Each instructor carried herself with the confidence that came from years of experience and proven skill.

As they drew closer, it became clear that all the instructors were female, just like the prospectives they had come to address.

Their ages varied, but each one radiated an aura of power that made the arena feel even more charged with energy.

The prospectives instinctively straightened their postures as the instructors approached.

The lead instructor was a tall woman with brown hair pulled back in an elegant style. Her presence commanded immediate attention, and the hostile murmuring between blocks gradually faded to respectful silence.

She positioned herself at the center of the main floor where all three blocks could see and hear her clearly.

The other instructors arranged themselves in a semi-circle behind their leader, creating an imposing display of academy authority.

Their synchronized movements suggested they had done this many times before, though definitely never with a man present in the stands.

The lead instructor stepped forward and raised her hand for complete silence. Her voice carried easily across the vast arena when she began to speak, enhanced by magical amplification that ensured every word reached even the furthest prospective.

"Welcome, prospectives of Blocks A, B, and C," she announced formally. "I am Instructor Valdris, and these are my colleagues from the main academy. Today marks a significant moment in your journey as prospective students."

She gestured toward each block in turn, acknowledging their presence equally.

"This is the first time all three side blocks have been gathered together in this arena. You have each proven yourselves worthy to reach this point through your individual efforts and dedication."

The prospectives listened intently, their earlier hostilities temporarily set aside in the face of official academy business. Ren noticed how quickly the atmosphere had shifted from confrontational to respectful and attentive.

Instructor Valdris continued her introduction with measured formality. "Each of your blocks has already successfully completed one special event during your stay here. The curse siege event tested your abilities under pressure and revealed much about your potential."

Murmurs of recognition rippled through the crowd as prospectives remembered their respective experiences with that challenging trial.

The curse siege had been difficult for everyone, regardless of which block they belonged to.

"However," the instructor’s voice grew more serious, "your time at the side blocks is not yet complete. There remains one final special event that will determine not only your individual rankings but also your placement opportunities at the main academy."

The arena fell completely silent as Instructor Valdris prepared to deliver the most important part of her announcement. Even the subtle magical lighting seemed to dim slightly, as if the arena itself was focusing attention on her words.

"This final special event will be unlike anything you have experienced before," she declared.

"Not only will it establish the final rankings of all prospective students, but the individual who achieves first place overall will receive something unprecedented."

She paused for dramatic effect, letting the anticipation build among the hundreds of gathered prospectives.

The silence stretched until it became almost unbearable, with everyone hanging on her next words.

"The overall champion will receive a guaranteed placement in the Imperial Class of the main academy," she announced, her voice carrying the weight of this extraordinary revelation.

The reaction was immediate and explosive. Gasps and excited whispers erupted from all three blocks as the prospectives processed what they had just heard.

This was beyond anything they had dared to hope for during their time at the side blocks.

Ren felt his own pulse quicken at this announcement. The Imperial Class was legendary among prospective students, spoken of in hushed tones as the ultimate achievement.

No matter how well anyone performed at the side blocks, the highest placement they could normally expect was admission to the Supreme Class.

The Imperial Class was reserved for the most exceptional individuals, handpicked by the academy’s highest authorities.

These were students destined to become leaders, innovators, and the most powerful weavers of their generation. The exclusivity of this class made it seem almost mythical to regular prospectives.

But now, one of them would have the opportunity to join those elite ranks!

The chance to bypass the normal limitations and leap directly into the most prestigious class in the entire academy was beyond anything they had imagined possible.

Ren observed the reactions of those around him with keen interest. Every prospective in the arena seemed electrified by this unprecedented opportunity.

The earlier inter-block hostilities now seemed trivial compared to the prize that had just been revealed.

He turned his attention to Mirabella and was struck by the intensity of her reaction.

Her normally composed expression had transformed into something almost fanatic in its focus. Her eyes blazed with an ambition so fierce it was almost frightening to witness.

Mirabella’s entire body seemed to vibrate with determination and desire. This opportunity represented everything she had been working toward, and more.

Her hands were clenched into fists at her sides, and her breathing had become noticeably deeper and more controlled.

Even Lia, usually so cheerful and carefree, was paying rapt attention to every word.

Her typical bouncy energy had been replaced by serious concentration as she absorbed the magnitude of what was being offered. Her eyes were wide with possibility and growing determination.

The transformation in both his companions reflected what was happening throughout the arena.

Every prospective was suddenly seeing their future in an entirely new light. The Imperial Class placement had changed everything about the stakes they were playing for.

Ren himself felt a surge of determination unlike anything he had experienced before. This was exactly the kind of challenge that appealed to his competitive nature and analytical mind.

The opportunity to prove himself against the best from all three blocks was irresistible. But even more was the chance to gain more resources!

Resources meant strength, and in this world where he had no one, he could only rely on himself!

Instructor Valdris allowed the excitement to build for several moments before raising her hand for silence once again.

She had more crucial information to share, and the prospectives needed to understand exactly what they would be facing.

"The final special event will take the form of an Inter Block Tournament," she announced. "This competition will begin in exactly five days, giving you time to prepare for what lies ahead."

The word ’tournament’ sent another wave of anticipation through the crowd. This suggested direct competition between individuals rather than the group challenges they had faced during the curse siege event.

"The tournament will span three full days," the instructor continued. "It will test every aspect of your abilities, from combat skills to magical knowledge, strategic thinking to leadership potential. Only the most well-rounded and capable individuals will advance to the final stages."

She gestured toward the magnificent arena surrounding them. "This space will serve as the primary venue for the tournament. The multiple floors and varied environments will allow for different types of challenges and competitions."

The prospectives began looking around the arena with new understanding. What had seemed like an impressive but mysterious space now revealed itself as a carefully designed tournament venue.

Each of the smaller floors likely served specific purposes in the upcoming competition.

"The tournament structure will be announced in detail on the day it begins," Instructor Valdris explained.

"However, I can tell you that it will accommodate competitors from all three blocks equally, with advancement based purely on individual merit and performance."

The instructor’s expression grew more serious as she moved on to discuss the rewards and consequences of the tournament results. This information would determine how desperately each prospective would fight for advancement.

"While the top one hundred prospectives would each get rewarded handsomely, the top ten finishers in the tournament will each receive a Rare grade magical treasure," she announced.

"These items are specifically chosen to boost power levels significantly and will greatly assist in breaking through to the next stage of your development."

Excited murmurs rippled through the crowd at this news. Rare grade magical treasures were incredibly valuable and difficult to obtain.

For prospective students, such items could mean the difference between struggling with advancement and achieving breakthrough smoothly.

"However," the instructor continued, "there are additional special rewards for those who place higher than tenth position.

"The exact nature of these rewards will be revealed as the tournament progresses, but I assure you they are proportional to the achievement they represent."

This hint at even greater rewards for higher placements added another layer of motivation for the prospectives.

Not only was the Imperial Class placement at stake for first place, but clearly significant benefits awaited anyone who could achieve a top ranking.

The instructor looked around the arena one final time, making eye contact with prospectives from all three blocks.

"You now have five days to prepare yourselves for the most important challenge of your academy careers. Use this time wisely."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 64: Protection Request.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With her announcement complete, Instructor Valdris stepped back and gestured toward the three entrances they had all used to enter the arena.

"You are now dismissed to return to your respective blocks. Prepare well, and may the most worthy individual claim the ultimate prize."

The instructors began filing back up the grand staircase, which would presumably disappear again once they had departed. Their departure was as coordinated and impressive as their arrival had been.

The prospectives remained in the arena for several moments, still processing everything they had learned.

The magnitude of the opportunity and the intensity of the upcoming competition were almost overwhelming to contemplate.

Gradually, the three blocks began moving toward their respective exits.

The earlier hostilities had been replaced by a different kind of tension; the awareness that they would soon be competing directly against each other for the most prestigious prize any of them had ever had the chance to win.

As Block B prospectives filed toward their entrance, conversations buzzed with excitement and nervous energy.

Everyone was already beginning to plan their preparation strategies and consider their chances in the upcoming tournament.

They had just five days to prepare themselves and ensure they were ready enough to stand out amidst the other thousands of prospectives!

Ren walked among his block companions but found his mind already racing ahead to the challenges that awaited.

The Inter Block Tournament represented everything he had been working toward, and couldn’t have come at the right time.

By the time Ren emerged from the hidden arena and returned to the familiar corridors of Block B, his determination had crystallized into clear purpose.

The Imperial Class placement wasn’t just an opportunity, it was now his primary objective.

He bid quick farewells to Mirabella and Lia, both of whom seemed lost in their own thoughts about the tournament announcement.

The three of them would likely spend the next five days in intensive preparation, each pursuing their own path toward readiness.

Especially Mirabella, she barely gave them a nod of acknowledgment before vanishing into the distance at top speed.

Her determination was almost frightening!

Without hesitation, Ren headed directly toward the training areas as well. Five days wasn’t much time to prepare for such a significant competition, but he intended to use every available moment to maximize his chances of success.

The training facilities would become his second home over the next few days. Every skill he possessed would need to be sharpened, every weakness addressed, and every advantage explored.

The Inter Block Tournament would demand everything he had to give, and he was determined to be ready.

.....

Somewhere on the surface...

The Lightweave family’s private sanctum existed in a realm well protected, accessible only through ancient weaving techniques passed down through generations.

Crystalline walls stretched impossibly high, their surfaces reflecting infinite patterns of light that danced and shifted with each breath taken within the sacred space.

The air itself seemed to shimmer with power, charged with magical energy that made every hair stand on end.

At the center of this magnificent chamber stood a circular table carved from a single piece of prismatic crystal.

The table’s surface contained swirling galaxies of light that moved in slow, hypnotic patterns, each one representing different streams of information flowing through the sovereignty’s political networks.

Around this table sat seven women, each one a pillar of power within the Lightweave family hierarchy.

Lady Seraphina Lightweave occupied the seat of honor, her silver hair cascading down her shoulders like liquid moonlight.

Her eyes held the depth of centuries, and when she spoke, reality itself seemed to bend slightly to accommodate her words.

She wore robes of pure white that seemed to generate their own illumination, marking her as the family’s current matriarch.

The other six women represented different branches and specializations within the family structure.

Lady Celeste managed their intelligence networks, her fingers constantly weaving small patterns in the air that sent messages across vast distances.

Lady Aurora controlled their financial interests, her mind calculating profit margins and political investments with mechanical precision.

Lady Nova supervised their magical research, her eyes glowing faintly with the power of experimental techniques.

Each woman possessed the distinctive Lightweave features: pale skin that seemed to glow from within, hair in various shades of silver and white, and eyes that shifted color depending on the light.

Their beauty was otherworldly, enhanced by generations of magical bloodline refinement that had elevated them beyond normal human limitations.

Chime!

A gentle chime echoed through the sanctum as the dimensional barriers parted to admit their guests.

Lady Miranda Frostweave entered first, her bearing regal despite her youth, followed by three elder women whose presence commanded immediate respect.

The temperature in the chamber dropped noticeably as their ice-aligned magic interacted with the light-based energies already present.

Lady Evangeline Frostweave, Miranda’s grandmother and the family’s senior representative, moved with the grace of winter itself.

Her white hair was styled in an elaborate arrangement that seemed to contain actual snowflakes that never melted.

Her dress appeared to be woven from frozen starlight, creating patterns that shifted like ice crystals forming on a window.

Behind her walked Lady Cordelia and Lady Isadora, both aunts to young Miranda and formidable weavers in their own right.

Their synchronized movements spoke of decades working together in perfect harmony, their magic so intertwined that they often finished each other’s sentences without realizing it.

The Frostweave delegation took their seats at the crystal table with practiced elegance.

The mixing of light and ice magic created spectacular visual effects as their energies swirled together, forming aurora-like patterns in the air above the table’s surface.

Lady Seraphina inclined her head respectfully toward her counterparts. "Welcome, sisters of the Neutral Faction," she said, her voice carrying harmonics that resonated through the crystalline walls.

"We gather today to discuss matters of utmost importance to our shared interests."

Lady Evangeline’s ice-blue eyes studied the swirling patterns in the crystal table’s surface.

"The young man grows stronger each day," she observed, her breath creating small clouds despite the chamber’s magical climate control. "His progress exceeds even our most optimistic projections."

"Indeed," Lady Seraphina agreed, gesturing subtly so that new images appeared in the table’s depths.

"Our sources within the side blocks report that his magical development continues to accelerate. The tournament announcement has only intensified his training regimen."

The images showed Ren in various training scenarios, his silver eyes focused with determination as he practiced his techniques and weaves.

The women studied these scenes with the intensity of scientists observing a rare phenomenon, noting every detail of his progress and potential.

Lady Celeste leaned forward, her fingers weaving information streams that updated the visual display.

"However, we must consider the broader implications of his unprecedented nature. The first male weaver in recorded surface history will inevitably attract attention from all factions."

Lady Aurora nodded gravely. "The Traditionalists view him as an aberration to be eliminated. The Progressives see him as a tool for advancing their agenda. We alone recognize his true value as a stabilizing force for the neutral position."

Young Miranda listened intently to the discussion, though her expression remained carefully neutral.

Her recent observations of Mirabella’s interactions with Ren had given her unique insights into his character and capabilities, information that would prove valuable to her family’s strategic planning.

Lady Nova spoke up from her position at the table. "Our intelligence networks have detected increased activity from the Bloodweave faction. They appear to be taking a more active interest in the boy’s development than we initially anticipated."

Lady Seraphina’s expression grew more serious as she prepared to reveal the true purpose of this gathering.

"Which brings us to today’s primary agenda," she said, her voice dropping to a tone that commanded absolute attention. "We have determined that additional protective measures are necessary."

She gestured, and the crystal table’s surface shifted to display a complex web of connections and relationships.

"However, convincing our allies to implement these measures requires... compelling evidence of their necessity."

Lady Evangeline’s eyes narrowed slightly as she began to understand the implications. "What kind of evidence are you proposing to provide?"

"The kind that will ensure young Ren receives the protection he deserves," Lady Celeste answered smoothly.

"We have prepared a series of incidents that will demonstrate the very real threats he faces from traditionalist elements."

The images in the table shifted to show detailed plans and prepared evidence.

Lady Seraphina began explaining each element with the precision of a master strategist outlining a complex campaign.

"First, we have arranged for the protective formations around the side blocks to detect mysterious figures near the dormitories," she said.

"These will be our own agents, carefully disguised and employing Bloodweave magical signatures to create authentic readings."

Lady Aurora continued the explanation. "Second, we have prepared intercepted communications that discuss Ren’s ’anomalous nature’ in threatening terms.

These messages will appear to originate from known Bloodweave operatives and will contain enough accurate information to seem genuine."

Lady Nova added her contribution. "Third, our intelligence network will report on traditionalist meetings where Ren’s elimination is allegedly discussed. We have prepared transcripts and witness testimonies that will paint a convincing picture of organized opposition."

Lady Evangeline studied the proposed evidence with the analytical mind that had made her one of the most respected figures in the Neutral Faction.

"You’re asking us to base our protective requests on fabricated threats," she said carefully.

"We’re asking you to recognize that the threats are very real, even if the specific incidents are... enhanced for clarity," Lady Seraphina replied diplomatically.

"The Traditionalists truly do view Ren as a threat to their worldview. We’re simply providing concrete evidence of what they would do if given the opportunity."

Lady Cordelia spoke for the first time since arriving. "The boy’s safety is paramount," she said, her voice carrying the chill of absolute conviction.

"If creating false evidence ensures his protection, then perhaps the deception serves a greater truth."

Lady Isadora nodded in agreement. "The stakes are too high to rely on hope and good intentions. If we wait for genuine threats to materialize, we may be too late to prevent tragedy."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 65: Breaking Through To The Second Layer! (1)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The training room was completely silent except for the soft sound of Ren’s steady breathing. He lay flat on his back on the smooth floor, his arms relaxed at his sides and his silver eyes closed in deep concentration.

The room around him was dimly lit by magical crystals that provided just enough light to see by without being distracting.

Even after nearly a month in this magical world, Ren still couldn’t get comfortable sitting in the lotus position like he had seen in movies and novels back home.

Every time he tried to cross his legs and sit upright for meditation, his back would start aching and his legs would fall asleep.

Lying on his back felt much more natural and allowed him to focus completely on his mental training.

The smooth floor was surprisingly comfortable against his back. The academy’s training rooms were designed for long practice sessions, and even the floors had been made to provide proper support for extended meditation.

Small grooves carved into the stone helped with air circulation and temperature control.

Ren had been in this position for over an hour already. His breathing was slow and controlled, each breath carefully measured to maintain the perfect state of relaxation needed for deep magical practice.

His mind was completely focused inward, exploring the complex patterns of magical energy that flowed through his body and consciousness.

Suddenly, his calm expression changed dramatically. His brow furrowed with intense concentration, and beads of sweat began forming on his forehead.

Within moments, the sweat was pouring from his skin like he had just finished running a marathon in blazing heat.

The perspiration soaked through his training clothes and began pooling on the stone floor around him.

His breathing became slightly more labored as his body responded to the incredible mental strain he was putting himself through.

Every muscle in his body tensed as he pushed his consciousness to its absolute limits.

It had been exactly one day since all the prospectives had been informed about the upcoming Inter Block Tournament.

The announcement had changed everything for Ren, giving him a clear goal and timeline to work toward.

The promise of Imperial Class placement for the overall winner had ignited a determination in him that burned stronger than ever before.

In just twenty-four hours, he had already made remarkable progress in his magical development.

His training schedule had become even more intense, with longer meditation sessions and more focused practice periods. Every moment was dedicated to pushing himself closer to his maximum potential.

The tournament would test every aspect of his abilities, and he knew that his current level wouldn’t be enough to compete with the best prospectives from all three blocks.

He needed to achieve a breakthrough that would give him a significant advantage over his competition.

Time was running short, and he couldn’t afford to waste a single opportunity for improvement.

His mental range had grown steadily over the past month of training. Through careful practice and gradual expansion, he had finally reached the theoretical limit for his current stage.

The progress felt good, but he knew it still wasn’t enough for what lay ahead.

The Adept stage that he had reached allowed for a maximum mental range of ten meters.

This meant he could now sense and manipulate the threads of the Loom within a ten-meter radius around his body.

For most weavers, this represented a significant achievement that took several weeks or even months to accomplish.

But Ren wasn’t satisfied with being like most weavers. The tournament had shown him that he needed to be exceptional, not just competent.

The Imperial Class placement would only go to someone who could demonstrate abilities far beyond the normal range of prospective students.

Instead of attempting to break through to the next stage of magical development, Ren had made a different strategic decision.

Rather than trying to advance from Adept to Master stage, he was attempting something much more difficult and potentially much more rewarding.

He was trying to break through to the second layer of the Loom itself.

The Loom existed in multiple layers, each one deeper and more complex than the last.

Most weavers spent their entire careers working within the first layer, never even attempting to access the deeper levels.

The second layer required not just more power, but a fundamentally different understanding of how the Loom operated.

Not to mention, it usually slowed down the speed of advancement by a lot.

Ren knew that Mirabella had achieved access to the second layer about a week ago. Her victory over him in their battle had demonstrated the power that could only come from this deeper level of magical manipulation.

She had been able to cast weaves and create effects that were simply impossible from the first layer alone.

He was also certain that some of the other top prospectives from Blocks A and C had likely achieved the same breakthrough.

The most talented individuals from each block would naturally push themselves to reach this advanced level before the tournament.

Without access to the second layer, he would be at a severe disadvantage against these elite competitors.

But there was another reason for his choice that went beyond simple competition.

If he could manage to break through to the Master stage while already having access to the second layer of the Loom, his abilities would be dramatically enhanced compared to normal Master stage weavers.

A typical Master stage weaver operating in the first layer of the Loom had a maximum range of fifty meters.

But a Master stage weaver with access to the second layer could achieve one hundred meters of range in the first layer, plus an additional fifty meters of range in the second layer.

This was how the truly elite weavers gradually widened the gap between themselves and ordinary practitioners.

The mathematics of magical development were clear and unforgiving. Each advancement in both stage and layer created multiplicative rather than additive improvements.

The difference between good and exceptional weavers wasn’t just about talent or hard work, but about understanding how to maximize these multiplicative effects.

Ren spread his mental range out to its absolute maximum extent of ten meters.

It was time to break through to the second layer!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 66: Breaking Through To The Second Layer! (2)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

He could feel the magical threads of the Loom stretching in all directions around him, a complex web of threads that responded to his conscious will.

The sensation was like having invisible hands that could reach out and touch the fundamental forces of reality.

Now came the incredibly difficult part. He needed to compress all of this expanded mental range back down to a single, tiny point of concentrated consciousness.

The technique required him to gather every bit of his mental energy and focus it into the smallest possible area while maintaining perfect control.

The process was like trying to force an ocean through a drinking straw. His expanded consciousness naturally wanted to remain spread out, and compressing it created enormous internal pressure.

His brain felt like it was being squeezed in a vice, and waves of pain shot through his skull with each attempt.

Sweat continued pouring from his body as he struggled with the compression. His muscles trembled with the effort of maintaining perfect stillness while his mind underwent this incredible strain.

The magical crystals in the room began flickering as his intense mental activity interfered with their stable energy patterns.

He held the compressed point of consciousness for as long as possible, then tried to push it deeper into the structure of the Loom itself.

The first layer felt solid and familiar, but beyond it lay something else entirely. The second layer was like trying to see through thick fog, barely visible and constantly shifting.

Each attempt to penetrate deeper resulted in immediate failure.

The compressed point of his mental range would hit what felt like an invisible barrier and bounce back, leaving him exhausted and frustrated.

His head pounded with the aftereffects of the intense concentration required for each try.

But Ren refused to give up. He knew that this breakthrough was essential for his success in the tournament.

Every failed attempt taught him something new about the process, and he could feel himself getting closer to understanding what was required.

The hours passed in a blur of intense concentration and repeated failure.

Ren’s body remained motionless on the training room floor, but his mind was engaged in the most difficult battle of his life.

Each attempt to break through to the second layer required him to push his consciousness beyond what seemed physically possible.

His training clothes were completely soaked with sweat, and small puddles had formed on the stone floor around him.

The magical crystals continued to flicker and dim as his mental efforts created waves of interference throughout the room.

Any observer would have thought he was seriously ill or injured based on his physical condition.

But internally, Ren could feel progress being made. Each failed attempt brought him slightly closer to understanding the exact technique required.

The barrier between the layers wasn’t solid like a wall, but more like a membrane that needed to be approached in just the right way.

The compression technique was becoming easier with practice, though it still required enormous effort and concentration.

He could now gather his mental range into a focused point more quickly and maintain it for longer periods.

The pain in his head had become a constant companion, but he had learned to work through it.

As the hours continued to pass, Ren began to notice subtle changes in how the Loom responded to his probing attempts.

The second layer seemed to be becoming slightly more visible, like trying to focus on something in the distance that was slowly getting clearer.

There were moments when he could almost sense the deeper patterns that lay beyond the first layer.

His determination never wavered despite the physical and mental exhaustion building up in his body.

The importance of this breakthrough was too great to allow temporary discomfort to stop him. Every prospective in the tournament would be pushing themselves to their limits, and he needed to push even further.

When Ren finally took a break for food and rest, he had been practicing for nearly eighteen hours straight.

His body was completely drained, and his mind felt like it had been put through a blender. But he had made measurable progress, and that gave him hope for continued improvement.

After a few hours of sleep and a quick meal, he was ready to continue.

The second day of attempts began with renewed energy and refined technique.

His understanding of the compression process had improved significantly, and he could now achieve the focused point of consciousness more efficiently.

But the breakthrough itself remained elusive. The second layer of the Loom continued to resist his attempts to penetrate its mysteries.

Each failure was becoming more frustrating as he came tantalizingly close to success but couldn’t quite achieve the final push needed.

The physical strain was taking its toll on his body. His muscles ached from maintaining the same position for so many hours, and his head throbbed with constant pain from the intense mental effort.

By the afternoon of the second day, Ren was beginning to feel truly miserable.

The combination of physical discomfort, mental exhaustion, and repeated failure was testing his resolve like nothing ever had before.

Part of him wondered if he was attempting something beyond his current capabilities.

But giving up wasn’t an option. He forced himself to continue despite the growing urge to quit and try a different approach.

As evening approached on the second day, Ren prepared for what he hoped would be his final series of attempts.

His body was pushed to its absolute limits, and he knew he couldn’t maintain this level of intensity much longer.

If he couldn’t achieve the breakthrough soon, he would need to abandon this approach and find another way to prepare for the tournament.

He spread his mental range to its maximum extent one more time, feeling the familiar web of magical threads extending ten meters in all directions.

The compression technique had become almost automatic after so many hours of practice. His consciousness gathered itself into a tight, focused point with practiced efficiency.

This time, something felt different. The compressed point of his mental range seemed more stable and concentrated than ever before.

The pain in his head was still intense, but it felt more controlled and purposeful. He had finally achieved the perfect balance of compression and focus needed for the breakthrough attempt!

He directed the focused point toward the barrier between the layers with careful precision.

Instead of trying to force his way through with brute mental strength, he approached the membrane-like boundary with delicate probing motions.

The technique required a combination of power and finesse that had taken him nearly two days to develop.

For a moment, nothing happened. The familiar resistance met his mental probe, and he prepared for another failure.

But then, almost imperceptibly, something began to change. The barrier started to give way, not breaking but rather opening like a door that had finally found the right key.

His compressed mental range slipped through the opening and suddenly expanded into an entirely new realm of sensation!

The second layer of the Loom revealed itself in all its complex glory, a vast network of deeper patterns and more fundamental threads than anything he had ever experienced!

It was absolutely beautiful!

The moment Ren’s consciousness fully entered the second layer of the Loom, the training room erupted in spectacular magical phenomena.

A brilliant formation of light appeared beneath his prone body, spreading outward in intricate geometric patterns that covered several meters of the floor around him.

The formation pulsed with colors that seemed to exist beyond the normal spectrum of light.

Ancient symbols and modern magical equations rotated slowly within the glowing pattern, representing the deep relationships that governed the loom at its most fundamental level.

The sight was breathtakingly beautiful and profoundly complex.

Ren’s body remained motionless, but every cell seemed to be vibrating with newfound energy.

The exhaustion and pain that had been building for two days suddenly disappeared, replaced by a sense of clarity and power that was unlike anything he had ever experienced.

He had done it!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 67: Tournament Preparations.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The training hall buzzed with an almost electric energy as Ren settled into his meditation position.

The breakthrough to the second layer of the loom still felt surreal, like touching a dream that had suddenly become solid reality.

He could sense the difference immediately; the threads that danced through his consciousness were thicker now, more substantial, humming with a power that made his fingertips tingle even when he wasn’t actively weaving.

What’s more, he could sense the threads in the second layer at a radius of approximately 5 meters!

That was already insane considering he just broke through!

But power without control was just destruction waiting to happen. Ren had learned that lesson the hard way during his soul seizure. Now, he needed to master what he had gained before reaching for more.

The theoretical knowledge swirled through his mind like water finding its course.

A weave cast in the second layer carried twice the strength of the same pattern woven in the first layer.

The mathematics of it fascinated him, if he could reach the third layer someday, that same weave would be four times stronger than its first-layer counterpart!

At the same stage!

But the real revelation lay in understanding range dynamics.

When he cast a space lock weave at ten meters in the first layer, it generated a hypothetical force of one hundred units.

In the second layer, that same force could be achieved at just five meters range!

The implications made his pulse quicken. If he used the full ten-meter range while weaving in the second layer, the force would double to two hundred units.

Same stage, same fundamental technique, but the layer difference created an exponential leap in capability.

"Theory is one thing," Ren muttered to himself, flexing his fingers. "Practice is where dreams either soar or crash."

The threads in the second layer resisted him like thick honey compared to the flowing water of the first layer.

Each attempt to grasp and manipulate them required more concentration, more precise control.

His first tries at weaving the space lock pattern ended in frustrated tangles of energy that dissipated harmlessly but left him mentally drained.

Hours passed in patient repetition. Sweat beaded on his forehead as he fought to maintain the delicate balance between force and finesse.

At night, the evolution forge worked tirelessly within him, its abilities somehow making the impossible feel merely difficult.

By the end of the first day, he managed his first successful second-layer space lock weave.

The sensation was intoxicating; power flowing through the pattern like lightning through storm clouds.

When he tested it against a practice dummy, the binding force was so strong it actually cracked the reinforced training equipment!

"Twice as strong," he whispered, staring at the damaged dummy with something between awe and concern. "The masters weren’t exaggerating."

The second day brought focus to his mind blast weave. This pattern had always been trickier, requiring not just thread manipulation but also precise mental targeting. In the second layer, the complexity multiplied exponentially.

The threads seemed to have their own will, slipping away from his mental grasp like mischievous spirits.

Each failed attempt left him with a pounding headache that reminded him why most weavers took time to adapt to higher layers.

He was pushing the boundaries of what should be possible in such a short timeframe.
