"Twenty-five qualified, forty-seven called," the witch announced after another series of selections.

The mathematics were becoming brutal. Nearly half of those called had failed to identify their patterns correctly or within the time limit.

The seemingly simple memory test was proving more psychologically demanding than the complex magical trials that had preceded it.

More names echoed across the arena. Success and failure alternated with devastating randomness that highlighted how crucial those first few seconds of seat memorization had been days ago.

"Thirty two qualified," the witch declared with finality after the seventy-second candidate had been called. "The memory phase is complete."

The final count was staggering—seventy-two candidates had been called before thirty two successful selections were achieved.

The absurd efficiency of the method meant that natural ability, not calling order, had ultimately determined advancement, but the psychological pressure had been enormous for everyone involved.

The absurd efficiency of the method meant that not just natural ability, but calling order as well, had ultimately determined advancement, but the psychological pressure had been enormous for everyone involved.

The witch turned toward the eliminated candidates with an expression that mixed sympathy with practical finality.

"To those who did not advance," she addressed them with maternal warmth, "remember that your academy journey is far from over. Excellence takes many forms, and your paths will lead to achievements that may ultimately surpass those who continue today."

The brief consolation was genuine but quickly concluded.

The witch's attention shifted immediately to the sixteen successful candidates with an energy that suggested the real excitement was just beginning.

"Now we reach the trial's true purpose," she announced with visible anticipation. "The second phase will test the capabilities that truly matter—your ability to survive magical combat against your fellow prospectives would be tested under the scrutiny of the academy authorities."

Ren glanced around, surveying the stands around them.

Thousands of students filled the seats—every prospective who had been eliminated during previous trials, and what appeared to be some academy, students, elders and distinguished visitors whose presence suggested the final trial's significance extended far beyond student evaluation.

"Single elimination tournament," the witch announced with obvious delight. "Thirty one matches, one winner, one slot in the Imperial Academy's most exclusive programs."

"The first match," the witch continued with theatrical satisfaction, "will feature Sertina of Block C."

A girl stepped forward from the group of successful candidates. Ren recognized her immediately—the enigmatic student known throughout the side blocks simply as "Shadow."

Her pale features and dark clothing had made her memorable, but her magical capabilities had remained mysterious throughout the previous trials.

"Versus Cordelia of Block C," the witch added.

Another Block C student approached, her expression reflecting nervous determination.

Cordelia had advanced through competent but unremarkable performance, and being matched against Shadow in the opening round seemed almost unfairly challenging.

"All other candidates, please clear the arena floor," the witch instructed. "Our first duel will commence immediately."

As Ren and the other waiting candidates moved toward designated seating areas, he found himself studying Shadow with analytical interest.

Her combat reputation was based more on whispered rumors than observed performance, and this would be the first opportunity to see her capabilities displayed openly.

The two girls took positions in the arena's center, the vast space making them appear small and vulnerable beneath the watching thousands.

The contrast between Shadow's calm confidence and Cordelia's visible anxiety was striking even from a distance.

"Begin!" the witch's voice echoed across the arena with magical amplification.

Shadow disappeared.

Not gradually, not through conventional magical concealment, but instantly—dissolving into a terrifying puff of black smoke that spread across the arena floor like living darkness.

The transformation was so complete and sudden that even Ren's enhanced perception struggled to track what had occurred.

Cordelia's eyes widened in shock as she found herself alone in the center of the arena, surrounded by writhing shadows that seemed to move with predatory intelligence.

She raised her magical defenses frantically, but her defense found no attack at all.

Slice!

The next instant, a thin line of blood appeared across Cordelia's neck.

Not deep enough to cause serious injury, but clearly visible to every observer in the arena.

Shadow had struck with precision that demonstrated both incredible speed and perfect control, delivering a blow that could have been lethal but chose mercy instead.

The entire arena fell silent. Thousands of observers sat in stunned quiet as they processed what had just occurred.

Most hadn't even seen Shadow's attack—only the result that proved Cordelia's defeat beyond any doubt.

But Ren's eyes narrowed as his enhanced perception reconstructed the sequence of events.

He had seen enough to understand what Shadow had accomplished, and the implications were deeply concerning.

The speed she had demonstrated wasn't just exceptional—it was beyond anything that should have been possible for a student at their level of training. The precision of her strike suggested combat experience that extended far beyond academic instruction.

Ren's analytical mind replayed the sequence with methodical precision, breaking down what his enhanced perception had captured while most observers saw only impossibility.

Shadow hadn't truly disappeared—she had merged with the arena's natural shadows, becoming one with the darkness cast by the magical lighting overhead.

But the real innovation wasn't the concealment technique itself.

When she had dissolved into black smoke, Ren had noticed something others missed: her shadow on the arena floor had remained distinct and separate from the spreading darkness.

While her physical form became undetectable, her shadow had moved independently across the stone surface like a living entity.

Most remarkably, he had seen her shadow approach Cordelia's shadow from behind—two dark silhouettes converging on the arena floor while Cordelia frantically searched for an opponent who seemed to have vanished entirely.

The strike itself had been devastating in its simplicity. Shadow hadn't attacked Cordelia's physical body directly.

Instead, she had sliced at Cordelia's shadow with what appeared to be a blade of concentrated darkness.

All while still being in her own shadow form!

The wound that appeared on Cordelia's neck was the result of sympathetic magical connection between person and shadow.

The slight delay between the strike on the shadow and the woikd being inflicted in real time was proof that she had truly struck the shadow and not her real body that was merely being reflected.

By attacking the shadow, Shadow had inflicted real damage on her opponent's physical form—a technique that bypassed conventional magical defenses entirely because most protective weaves focused on direct physical or magical attacks.

The implications were staggering. This wasn't just advanced magic—it was combat methodology that operated on principles most prospectives had never encountered.

He had no idea if it was a weave or a rune ability.

Shadow magic at this level required understanding of dimensional manipulation, sympathetic magical theory, and combat application that suggested training far beyond standard academy curriculum.

More concerning was the precision required. One miscalculated strike at the shadow could have severed arteries in the physical body.

Shadow had demonstrated the ability to inflict precisely measured damage through techniques that most mages would consider theoretical at best.

She wasn't just dangerous—she was operating with knowledge and capabilities that made her a threat to anyone who faced her in direct combat.

This tournament had just become far more lethal than anyone had anticipated.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 93 - 93: Legacy Art!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The arena floor was still being cleared of the dissipating shadows from the previous match when the witch's voice rang out with renewed energy.

"Our second match," she announced with theatrical satisfaction, "will feature Mirabella of Block B."

Ren felt his attention sharpen as his rival stepped forward from the group of remaining candidates. Mirabella moved with her characteristic composed grace, her silver hair catching the magical lighting as she approached the center of the arena.

She looked completely calm and composed. There was no ounce of fear or nervousness on her face.

"Versus Hilda of Block A," the witch continued.

A second figure emerged from the candidates, and Ren found himself genuinely surprised by her appearance.

Where Mirabella represented elegant beauty enhanced by high class sophistication, Hilda embodied something entirely different. She was a striking combination of classical beauty and martial presence that commanded immediate attention.

Hilda's auburn hair fell in thick waves that seemed to catch and reflect light like burnished copper. Her emerald eyes held depths that suggested both intelligence and something more primal; a warrior's instinct that most academy students never developed.

Her features were sharp and perfectly symmetrical, creating the kind of beauty that would have made her memorable even without the unique weapon she carried. Especially considering how curvy and toned she was.

The halberd in her hands was a masterwork of both craftsmanship and magical enhancement. The blade gleamed with inner light that suggested enchantments beyond standard academy equipment, while the shaft appeared to be carved from some exotic wood that seemed to pulse with its own magical energy.

The weapon was nearly as tall as Hilda herself, yet she wielded it with the casual ease of someone who had trained with it for years.

Her entire presence radiated competence and controlled lethality that made even Ren reconsider his assessment of Mirabella's chances.

While his friend possessed superior analytical capabilities and strategic thinking, Hilda appeared to be a pure combatant whose martial skills might prove overwhelming in direct confrontation.

Even from his position in the candidate observation area, Ren could see the subtle differences in their approaches to the impending battle. Mirabella was analyzing, calculating, preparing to implement strategic solutions to tactical problems.

Hilda was simply ready—poised to unleash martial capabilities that needed no complex planning to be devastatingly effective.

The thousands of observers in the arena stands had grown noticeably quieter as they absorbed the visual impact of two remarkably beautiful and obviously skilled combatants preparing to face each other in serious magical combat.

After Shadow's shocking display of lethal capabilities, everyone understood that these matches would test the absolute limits of the candidates' abilities. The tournament had reached a stage where each and every participant was unique and powerful in their own way.

It was definitely going to be another thrilling watch.

"Begin!" the witch commanded.

The next few seconds unfolded with dreamlike slowness that made every detail crystal clear despite the incredible speed of events. At least to Ren, it felt that way.

A dazzling, resplendent armor began manifesting around Mirabella's body. It was colored in ethereal blues and pristine whites that seemed to exist simultaneously in multiple dimensions.

The armor appeared to be crafted from crystallized starlight and frozen moonbeams, creating protection that was both impossibly beautiful and obviously formidable.

The manifestation was unlike anything Ren had ever witnessed. Where most magical enhancements were either practical or aesthetic, Mirabella's armor achieved both purposes with perfection that suggested capabilities far beyond standard academy training.

She looked like a warrior goddess descended from celestial realms, but the power radiating from her form was absolutely terrifying in its magnitude.

The arena temperature plummeted instantly. Ice began forming across every surface, not gradually, but with explosive speed that created a crackling symphony of crystallization.

Within heartbeats, the entire combat area had been transformed into a frozen landscape that sparkled with magical energy. It was a beautiful landscape of cold and biting chill.

Hilda had begun moving the moment combat commenced, her halberd spinning in complex patterns as she advanced toward her opponent with fluid grace that showcased years of martial training.

Her combat form was flawless, her approach tactically sound, her readiness to engage in close combat obvious and impressive. A dazzling red rune on her neck began pulsing with waves of power, a sign of her activating her rune's special ability.

But she never reached striking range.

Mirabella raised her hand with casual elegance that made the gesture appear almost lazy. The ice covering the arena responded instantly, flowing toward her position like liquid mercury despite its solid state.

Thousands of ice fragments converged at a single point above her palm, compressing and condensing with forces that defied physical laws.

The result was an ice needle no longer than a finger but possessing density that made it effectively indestructible.

Bang!

When Mirabella released it, the projectile moved faster than bullets, faster than most weaves, faster than anything Hilda could have anticipated or defended against. It broke the sound barrier!

The needle struck Hilda center mass before she could react. The impact didn't cause too much injury on impact—instead, it triggered an instantaneous freezing effect that encased her entire form in a block of ice so clear and perfect it resembled the finest crystal sculpture.

Hilda remained conscious within her frozen prison, her emerald eyes showing awareness but complete immobilization. She was unharmed but utterly defeated, trapped in ice that would require significant magical intervention to remove safely.

If Mirabella wanted her dead, she would be dead within an instant. That much was clear to all that beheld this move. There was no doubt about it.

The entire arena fell silent once again. After Shadow's shocking display of lethal precision, they had witnessed something equally impressive but fundamentally different.

This was raw magical power applied with absolute control and devastating effectiveness!

It was terrifying!

Ren found his hands gripping the edge of his observation platform with enough force to leave marks in the stone. Despite maintaining his characteristic calm expression, internally he was processing implications that extended far beyond this single combat demonstration.

The armor surrounding Mirabella wasn't just advanced magical enhancement—it was something far more significant and dangerous.

The way it had materialized, the perfection of its construction, the sheer magnitude of power it enabled, all pointed to capabilities that shouldn't exist among academy prospectives.

That armor was quite literally a legacy art!

The realization sent a chill through Ren that had nothing to do with the lingering cold from Mirabella's ice magic.

Legacy arts were techniques that transcended normal magical education, representing knowledge and power that connected directly to the most legendary figures in magical history.

If Mirabella possessed access to legacy art capabilities, then his assessment of the competition's difficulty had been catastrophically inadequate.

He wasn't just facing exceptionally trained prospectives, he was competing against individuals who wielded power that bordered on the mythical!

The tournament had revealed two candidates whose abilities exceeded normal parameters by enormous margins.

Shadow's dimensional manipulation and sympathetic magic suggested training that went far beyond academy curriculum. Mirabella's legacy art armor indicated access to magical knowledge that most people believed was purely historical.

As the ice began melting under the witch's dispelling magic and Hilda was carefully extracted from her crystalline prison, Ren realized that his own strategic thinking would need to evolve dramatically if he hoped to survive the remaining matches.

The competition wasn't just about magical skill or tactical intelligence anymore. It had become a contest between individuals who possessed capabilities that redefined what was possible for students at their level of training.

And he had no idea how many more surprises awaited among the remaining candidates.

The arena floor was still glistening with residual moisture from the melted ice when the witch's voice echoed once more across the vast space.

"Our third match," she announced with obvious anticipation, "will feature Ren of Block B."

Ren felt the familiar surge of analytical focus as he stepped forward, his mind already processing tactical considerations for whatever opponent awaited him.

After witnessing Shadow's dimensional manipulation and Mirabella's legacy art, he understood that conventional approaches would likely prove inadequate against whatever capabilities his next challenger possessed.

"Versus Lyra of Block A," the witch concluded with theatrical flourish.

The announcement sent a ripple of surprise through both the candidates and the thousands of observers filling the arena stands. Ren felt his own composure waver momentarily as he processed the unexpected matchup.

Lyra Amethyst stepped forward with the same graceful confidence she had displayed throughout the previous trials, but her expression reflected the complexity of their situation.

They had worked together during the team trial, had developed mutual respect for each other's capabilities, had achieved victory through collaborative excellence that transcended their original factional differences.

Now they were required to face each other in single combat where only one could advance.

The middle Amethyst sister's Oracle abilities had been crucial to their team's success, but more importantly, Ren had witnessed her analytical intelligence and adaptability under pressure.

She wasn't just a formidable opponent—she was someone whose strategic thinking operated at levels that few students ever achieved.

As they took their positions in the arena center, the psychological weight of the matchup was obvious to everyone present.

Two candidates who had proven their ability to surpass their peers now forced to demonstrate their individual capabilities against someone who understood their mental strengths and weaknesses intimately.

The silence in the arena was profound as thousands of observers waited to see how this unexpected confrontation would unfold.

"Begin!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 94 - 94: Ren Vs Lyra! (1)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The moment the witch's command echoed across the arena, both combatants moved with explosive precision.

Lyra immediately connected to the loom, her enhanced mental capabilities allowing her to weave with blazing speed.

The first layer threads of time magic responded instantly to her will, and the familiar glow of a time affiliated weave erupted in her violet eyes like twin stars.

The world around her slowed as her perception accelerated, revealing the branching possibilities of the next second with crystalline clarity.

This was a temporal sight weave and it allowed her to see the future!

In her enhanced vision, she saw Ren drawing his kusarigama while simultaneously beginning his own weave connection. But more importantly, she could see the attack pattern he was most likely to attempt—a diagonal approach with his weapon's chain extended for maximum reach.

Ren, for his part, had already begun his own rapid analysis. He observed Lyra's immediate connection to the time loom and recognized the telltale signs of predictive enhancement with sharp awareness.

Her positioning shifted subtly but decisively, preparing for an attack she couldn't possibly have anticipated through normal means.

'She's seeing ahead,' he realized with grim satisfaction. 'One second, maybe less. Not much, but enough to be dangerous.'

Instead of rushing forward as Lyra's temporal sight had predicted, Ren did something completely unexpected—he stopped moving entirely and began a second weave connection while maintaining the first.

His chaos mental sea allowed him to split his focus in ways that should have been impossible, preparing two different weaving approaches simultaneously with ruthless efficiency.

Lyra's temporal sight flickered with confusion. The probability stream she was observing suddenly became muddled as Ren's actions diverged from her predicted timeline.

Her enhanced cognition raced to recalculate, but the dual weave preparation was creating variables her predictive magic couldn't properly account for in such a short timeframe.

"Interesting," she called out, raising her crystal staff defensively with fluid grace. "You're adapting faster than my sight can track."

Ren smiled grimly as he completed his preparations with mechanical precision but he didn't reply. One second of foresight was only useful if your opponent stayed predictable.

He launched his first attack—not with his weapon, but with devastating weaves.

A Space Lock weave manifested violently around Lyra's position, creating invisible barriers that slammed into place with crushing force, restricting her movement in all directions. But simultaneously, he was preparing something infinitely more dangerous.

Lyra's temporal sight had given her just enough warning to begin reacting. She switched weaves with lightning expertise, abandoning her predictive magic in favor of Time Dilation.

The transition was rougher than she'd hoped since weaves weren't designed for seamless body enhancement, and the temporal magic fought against her physical form as it tried to alter her personal timestream.

Her perception of the world stuttered and slowed unevenly while her physical responses accelerated in jarring bursts. The imperfect enhancement still allowed her to analyze the space lock's formation, but the disorientation made identifying weaknesses more difficult than it should have been.

Instead of trying to break through the spatial barriers directly, she attempted a Temporal Echo with breathtaking speed.

The weave partially succeeded, creating flickering afterimages of herself, but the echoes were unstable and inconsistent—side effects of forcing temporal magic to interact with physical matter.

But Ren had anticipated this limitation perfectly! The moment she began her compromised movement, he released his second prepared weave with lethal intent.

"Mind Arrow!" he whispered with cold fury.

The attack was invisible, intangible, and absolutely unavoidable. Pure mental force lanced across the arena like a bolt of lightning, striking directly at Lyra's consciousness with precision that bypassed all physical defenses completely.

The effect was immediate and catastrophically devastating. Lyra's concentration shattered like glass as overwhelming pain exploded through her mind with volcanic intensity.

Her time dilation weave collapsed instantly, and she stumbled violently as a migraine of incredible intensity disrupted her enhanced cognitive processing!

For the first time in the battle, Lyra was operating without magical enhancement, and the disadvantage was immediately apparent.

Swoosh!

Ren closed the distance with his kusarigama explosively, the heavy weapon spinning in complex patterns that showcased years of dedicated training with deadly efficiency due to the evolution forge.

But Lyra was still an Amethyst, and elite training didn't disappear just because of pain. Fighting through the mental agony with iron determination, she managed to activate her Stage 1 rune.

And not just an activation, it was a resonance!

Roar!

The mirage of an Amethyst Temporal Wyrm materialized dramatically behind her—a crystalline dragon-like creature that existed partially outside normal time. Lyra had achieved resonance, and her abilities doubled in potency with earth-shaking power!

Her resonance special ability activated with overwhelming force. Despite the lingering effects of the mind arrow, Lyra's consciousness expanded to encompass tactical possibilities with supernatural clarity.

She could see not just what Ren was doing, but why he was doing it, and what he would likely do in the next few seconds. She called this ability Perfect prediction, and it turned her into an absolute monster!

The kusarigama strike that should have connected instead met empty air as Lyra moved with prescient grace, her body flowing around attacks with fluid precision. Her crystal staff blazed with light magic as she began her devastating counterattack.

"Prismatic Barrier, second layer," she intoned with commanding authority.

Walls of crystallized light erupted around Ren's position with explosive force. But these weren't just simple defensive constructs, they were ingeniously crafted trap components designed to create a tactical maze by manipulating light.

Ren found himself surrounded by reflective surfaces that turned his own spatial awareness against him viciously. Every direction he looked showed multiple reflections that made determining real space from magical illusion nearly impossible.

But instead of panicking, Ren analyzed the construction with ice-cold precision. The barriers were designed to confuse and disorient, but they had structural requirements that created exploitable weaknesses.

He didn't try to break through the barriers directly. Instead, he began weaving again with deadly purpose, preparing another mind arrow while using his spatial magic to locate the exact positions of the real barriers through dimensional sensing rather than visual confirmation.

With his space affinity boosting his spatial awareness, this sort of mental attack would still find it's way to Lyra whether she wanted it or not. This was a benefit of the mind path!

Lyra's Perfect Prediction warned her of his intent with crystal clarity, but the solution created devastating new problems.

To maintain the barriers, she needed to keep her light weave active. To defend against the mind arrow, she needed to prepare mental defenses. Her single-weave limitation meant she had to choose.

She chose offense with ruthless determination.

The barriers dissolved as she switched to a second-layer Light Lance weave. Concentrated radiance gathered at her staff's tip with blinding intensity. It was enough destructive power to pierce through most magical defenses and deal serious injury!

But the moment her barriers dropped, Ren was already moving with explosive speed.

His spatial magic let him pinpoint her exact location despite the lingering light-based disorientation effects, and his kusarigama's weighted chain extended in a complex throwing pattern that used the arena's dimensions to create an unavoidable attack vector.

Woosh!

Lyra's Perfect Prediction showed her the chain's trajectory with supernatural clarity, but it also revealed something terrifying—dodging conventionally would only move her into the weapon's secondary strike path.

The prediction was perfect, but the solution required something beyond normal capabilities. Ren had essentially made an unblockable move!

She made a desperate decision that required absolute confidence in her predictive abilities.

She stepped directly into the chain's path!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 95 - 95: Ren Vs Lyra! (2)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Lyra stepped into the chain's path, but she stepped into it exactly when and where the weapon would be at its maximum extension, with minimal kinetic energy remaining.

Her crystal staff intercepted the chain at precisely the right angle to deflect rather than absorb the impact, while her body moved in perfect harmony with the weapon's momentum to avoid injury.

The maneuver was so perfectly executed that it bordered on artistic brilliance, drawing thunderous gasps from the thousands of observers who could barely follow the combat's incredible speed.

But Ren had been expecting something extraordinary. The moment Lyra committed to her deflection, he activated abilities he hadn't yet revealed.

His Stage 1 umbral kinephantom panther rune activated with terrifying power. Momentum transfer engaged explosively!

All the kinetic energy from his kusarigama's deflection didn't dissipate, instead, it flowed into Ren's body with crushing force, supercharging his next movement with accumulated energy.

Without having to gather momentum, he launched himself forward with velocity that defied normal physics. His enhanced speed turned him into a blur of motion that even Lyra's Perfect Prediction struggled desperately to track.

But more crucially, he was weaving again with deadly intent. Another mind arrow was building with overwhelming power, but this time he was putting everything into it.

This was a second-layer mental weave with focused intent designed to overwhelm even enhanced cognitive defenses!

The moment the dazzling formation that signified the manipulation of the second layer of the loom appeared, the audience were both terrified and amazed. They almost couldn't believe their eyes!

This was the second layer!

Lyra saw the attack coming through her prescient abilities, but Perfect Prediction also showed her a terrifying truth—she couldn't dodge this combination.

The mind arrow would strike regardless of her physical position, and the mental damage would be severe enough to potentially cause unconsciousness.

Her only option was a desperate gambit that would require perfect execution and enormous risk.

She activated her Stage 2 rune with explosive determination.

Temporal Amplification surged through her magical systems like liquid fire, increasing the potency of all her time-based abilities by fifty percent. But more importantly, it allowed her to attempt something that should have been beyond her current capabilities.

"Temporal Shift, second layer," she called out with desperate intensity, pouring everything she had into the most dangerous time weave she knew.

The effect was localized temporal manipulation that briefly accelerated her personal timestream relative to the rest of reality.

But the weave fought viciously against her physical form, causing painful distortions as her body struggled to exist at a different temporal rate than the surrounding space.

For exactly two seconds of real time, Lyra existed in agonizing fast-forward, moving and thinking at rates that made the rest of the world appear frozen while her nervous system screamed in protest.

It essentially slowed down the time of the world, relative to her! This was both different and leagues above speeding up her time in relative to the world.

The mind arrow struck the space where she had been with devastating force, but Lyra was no longer occupying that temporal moment. She had shifted herself slightly forward in time, causing the attack to miss by existing in a different temporal frame.

But the technique came with enormous costs. The magical strain was tremendous, and maintaining temporal displacement while her body rejected the enhancement pushed her abilities to their absolute limits.

When she snapped back to normal temporal flow, she was already executing a complex multi-phase attack that Ren couldn't have anticipated.

Three Temporal echoes (clones) attempted to manifest simultaneously, but the strain from her previous temporal shift meant the echoes were unstable and flickering. Only two managed to achieve partial solidity, and both were clearly struggling to maintain coherence.

The weakened echoes began weaving different attacks with imperfect coordination: one preparing a Light Lance, the other attempting a Blinding Flash, while the real Lyra desperately tried to maintain both constructs.

Ren found himself facing what appeared to be three opponents, but his enhanced perception could detect the instability in the temporal constructs.

His analytical mind raced through options with lightning speed.

The echoes were real enough to weave, but they were deteriorating rapidly. The attacks were coming from different vectors, but they all originated from the same increasingly exhausted source.

Most importantly, maintaining even two unstable temporal duplicates was draining Lyra's mental sea reserves at an unsustainable rate.

Instead of trying to defend against all three attacks, Ren made a calculated decision that demonstrated why he had survived every previous challenge.

He targeted Lyra herself with ruthless precision.

While her temporal echoes prepared their attacks, Ren used his stage 2 Umbral Kinephantom Panther rune ability.

Instead of absorbing kinetic energy, he released an insane amount of momentum in a single explosive movement.

He didn't attack where Lyra was—he attacked where she had to be.

His kusarigama struck toward the exact spot where the real Lyra would be standing when her temporal echoes expired and her consciousness returned fully to her primary body.

The prediction required understanding her magical limitations, her physical positioning requirements, and the temporal mechanics governing her failing echo creation.

Fiendish whirl!

Howllll!

The weapon arrived precisely as Lyra's echoes collapsed and she materialized, completely drained from the temporal magic. She had no energy left to dodge or defend.

But instead of striking to injure, Ren demonstrated the control that separated elite combatants from simple brawlers.

The kusarigama's weighted end stopped exactly one inch from her throat, close enough that she could feel the weapon's heat but far enough away to avoid any actual harm.

"Yield," he said quietly, his voice carrying clearly across the suddenly silent arena.

Lyra looked down at the weapon positioned at her throat, then back at Ren's eyes with grudging respect. Her mental reserves were completely exhausted, and his positioning made escape absolutely impossible.

More importantly, she recognized the precision and restraint he had demonstrated. The attack could have been lethal, but he had chosen to end the battle with a clear demonstration of victory rather than inflicting injury on someone he respected.

"I yield," she said with quiet dignity.

The arena erupted in thunderous applause as both combatants stepped back and bowed to each other with mutual respect.

They had demonstrated magical combat at its highest levels—a combination of raw power, tactical brilliance, and personal honor that elevated competition into art.

But as Ren walked back toward the candidate observation area, he was already analyzing the implications of what he had been forced to reveal.

His momentum creation ability was now known, his dual weaving capability had been observed, and his tactical approach to exploiting temporal magic weaknesses was documented.

The remaining candidates would have valuable intelligence about his capabilities, and he would need to evolve his approach for the battles yet to come.

The tournament was far from over, and each victory made the next challenge more dangerous than the last.

Still, he was determined to see it to the end!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 96 - 96: Tough Matchup!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ren walked back to the candidate observation area with measured steps, his expression maintaining the same calm composure he had shown throughout the battle.

To the thousands of observers filling the arena, his victory over Lyra had appeared almost effortless to the viewers. It was a demonstration of superior tactical thinking that had systematically dismantled her formidable abilities.

But the reality was far more complex than appearances suggested.

The battle against Lyra had been anything but easy, requiring every ounce of his analytical capabilities and strategic experience to achieve victory while revealing as little as possible about his true abilities.

The advantages he had possessed were real but limited, requiring precise exploitation to be effective.

His mind affinity had indeed provided the key to countering Lyra's temporal magic, but only because he understood the fundamental relationship between mental clarity and complex magical weaving.

Most students would have attempted to overpower her predictive abilities through brute force, failing to recognize that her entire combat system depended on uninterrupted concentration.

But identifying the weakness was only the beginning.

Executing the strategy had required split-second timing, perfect positioning, and the ability to maintain multiple tactical considerations simultaneously while engaged in high-speed magical combat.

The dual weaving capability that his chaos mental sea provided had been crucial, but not in the obvious way.

While other observers had focused on his ability to cast two spells simultaneously, the real advantage lay in the psychological pressure it created.

Lyra's Perfect Prediction worked by calculating optimal responses to anticipated actions, but when faced with an opponent who could execute multiple unpredictable combinations, her enhanced cognition became overwhelmed with variables.

The mental warfare aspect had been even more complex than the magical combat.

Every move he made had been calculated not just for immediate tactical advantage, but for long-term strategic positioning.

He needed to demonstrate enough capability to advance while avoiding revealing the full extent of his abilities to future opponents.

The momentum creation technique he had used for his final attack was deliberately chosen for its psychological impact as much as its tactical effectiveness.

By demonstrating an ability that appeared to violate normal magical physics, he had established himself as someone whose capabilities extended beyond conventional analysis.

Future opponents would need to prepare for possibilities they couldn't predict, giving him crucial advantages in upcoming battles.

As Ren settled into position to observe the remaining first-round matches, he began processing intelligence about his potential future opponents with the same analytical precision he had applied to defeating Lyra.

"Vera Amethyst versus Marcella of Block C," the witch announced, her voice carrying obvious anticipation for the next demonstration of elite magical combat.

The youngest Amethyst sister stepped forward with characteristic directness, her movements flowing with the controlled grace that spoke of years of elite magical training.

Marcella, a prospective known for her methodical fighting style, approached with visible determination that suggested she understood the magnitude of the challenge she faced.

The moment combat began, Vera demonstrated why the Amethyst family was renowned throughout the surface.

Her water affinity manifested with devastating precision as she connected to the loom's second layer, manipulating water to create tactical advantages that most students couldn't even perceive.

But her true advantage came from her unique carved runes.

Her Stage 1 Azure Sea Serpent rune activated, and the familiar stat boosts flooded through her body—enhanced speed, improved magical capacity, and superior physical conditioning.

But more importantly, the rune's special ability activated: Aqueous Form.

Vera's left arm began shifting into liquid water while maintaining its basic structure and function.

The limb became semi-transparent and fluid, allowing her to bend and stretch it in ways that normal anatomy couldn't achieve.

When Marcella launched an earth projectile at her arm, it passed through the liquefied limb without causing damage, though Vera had to concentrate to maintain the transformation.

The mirage of an azure serpent appeared behind her as she achieved resonance, doubling the effectiveness of her transformation.

Now she could liquefy both arms simultaneously while maintaining better control over their density and form. It wasn't only her arms she could liquify either, she could do it for any limb.

Marcella attempted to establish range and use her earth weaves to create tactical advantages through systematic earth-based weave combinations.

Her approach was textbook perfect, but Vera's fighting style incorporated her unique ability in creative ways.

When Marcella created earth barriers for defense, Vera liquefied her arms to reach through narrow gaps, delivering strikes from her daggers at unexpected angles.

Her water-based weaving combined with her limited transformation ability created unpredictable attack patterns that were difficult to counter.

However, the ability came with clear limitations. Vera could only maintain the liquefied state for short periods before exhaustion set in, and transforming required significant concentration that left her vulnerable to coordinated attacks.

Marcella managed to exploit this weakness several times, forcing Vera to rely on conventional combat techniques for extended periods.

The engagement concluded when Vera used her aqueous form strategically, liquefying her blocking arm to avoid Marcella's earth-enhanced punch while simultaneously striking with her solid arm.

The technique was precise but clearly taxing, leaving Vera visibly drained despite her victory.

"Elena Amethyst versus Cassia of Block A," came the next announcement.

The eldest sister fought like a general commanding a battlefield, her space affinity creating tactical advantages through dimensional awareness while her analytical mind processed variables with mechanical efficiency.

Elena's Stage 1 Radiant Phoenix rune activated, flooding her body with enhanced magical recovery and combat endurance.

But the rune's special ability made her truly formidable: Phoenix Vision.

Her eyes began glowing with soft golden light as she gained the ability to see energy signatures and flows with perfect clarity.

This allowed her to track opponents through concealment, identify weak points in magical defenses, and predict attack patterns by watching magical energy buildup in her enemy's body.

Although this might seem like a pretty weak ability, it was actually quite insane. The ability to visualize energy itself was a broken ability for any  magical profession.

Not only could she literally see the loom with absurd clarity compared to the others, it also had a direct impact on her will, her ability to sense and manipulate the loom and even break through into deeper layers.

It was that broken!

Cassia possessed impressive capabilities, but Elena's enhanced perception provided crucial advantages.

She could see exactly where Cassia was preparing spells by observing energy patterns, giving her precious seconds to prepare counters.

When Elena achieved resonance, the golden phoenix mirage appeared behind her, extending her vision range and improving her ability to distinguish different energy signatures.

Combined with her space-based battlefield control, she positioned herself optimally by reading the arena's energy landscape.

Elena systematically dismantled Cassia's effectiveness by predicting attacks through energy observation and creating spatial disadvantages her opponent couldn't navigate.

The victory came through superior information gathering rather than overwhelming power.

"Sertina versus Morgana of Block A," the witch continued.

Shadow's second appearance was even more unsettling.

When Morgana used area-effect spells to counter stealth attacks, Shadow demonstrated she could manipulate darkness as tangible force.

The arena filled with writhing shadows that moved independently, creating tactical confusion.

Shadow's victory came when she emerged from solid stone itself, having used dimensional magic to move through the arena floor.

Her casual ease in bypassing every defensive measure suggested supernatural capabilities. Among the remaining prospectives, she was the one Ren felt was the most difficult to face.

Her abilities were simply too bizarre and unconventional. She was even more difficult to deal with than himself!

Several more matches followed. Daria of Block B defeated Helena through innovative fire techniques that overcame conventional wind magic training.

Lysandra proved her advancement was earned through methodical competence despite political connections.

When the first round concluded, sixteen candidates remained from thirty two.

The arena atmosphere intensified as observers processed what they'd witnessed—each remaining fighter possessed capabilities that could prove decisive.

There were unique and special in their own way, making predictions for the final winner very uncertain.

"The first round has concluded," the witch announced with satisfaction. "But now the real testing begins. The second round will determine who possesses excellence that transcends normal basic education."

Magical displays shifted to show new matchups, and Ren analyzed potential opponents. Every future battle would be against someone representing the highest levels of student achievement.

"The second round begins immediately," the witch continued. "Our first match features two candidates whose abilities represent the pinnacle of magical education and strategic thinking."

She paused dramatically as thousands awaited the announcement.

"Mirabella of Block B versus Elena Amethyst."

The announcement sent excitement through the stands as observers realized they would witness a battle between two candidates whose capabilities had consistently exceeded expectations.

Ren's analytical attention sharpened. Both possessed exceptional abilities operating at levels few students achieved, and their contrasting approaches would create a battle testing the limits of elite magical education.

It was time for the long awaited Neutral Faction Vs Traditionalist Faction battle!

Who would come out on top?

"Begin!"

Rumble!

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 97 - 97: Mirabella Vs Elena!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The arena floor cleared as Mirabella and Elena took their positions at opposite ends of the vast combat space.

The atmosphere was electric with anticipation; two daughters of legendary noble families, each representing the pinnacle of magical breeding and training, about to demonstrate why their bloodlines commanded respect across the surface.

Mirabella drew her single dagger with fluid grace, the weapon gleaming with inner frost as she channeled magic through its enhanced edge.

The air temperature around her dropped noticeably as she connected to the loom's second layer, ice crystals beginning to form in the air itself, suspended like deadly ornaments.

Elena's Stage 1 Radiant Phoenix rune activated, her eyes beginning to glow with golden light as her Phoenix Vision enhanced her perception.

She could see heat signatures, energy flows, and calculate precise distances—crucial for optimal portal placement within her line of sight.

"Begin!"

CRACK!

Mirabella opened with calculated aggression. Three ice shards materialized in the air around her, each one forming at insane speeds. She launched them in a spread pattern, not only aimed at Elena but also at the spaces where she might dodge.

These weren't simple projectiles. Each one carried the compressed density of her advanced ice manipulation, sharp enough to pierce steel and cold enough to freeze blood on contact.

Elena's response showcased the terrifying effectiveness of her enhanced Phoenix Vision. Her golden eyes tracked every icicle's trajectory with supernatural precision, reading the thermal patterns and energy flows that revealed their exact paths.

She wove spatial distortions with clinical efficiency, creating two portal pairs simultaneously, catching the outer shards and redirecting them back at converging angles. The middle shard she simply sidestepped, already calculating where Mirabella would move.

WHOOSH!

But Mirabella had never released control of her ice. The shards Elena had redirected suddenly changed trajectory mid-flight, curving around to attack her from new angles!

Upon activation of her stage 3 rune, Mirabella could easily regain or maintain control of her constructs as long as they remained within thirty feet.

Her magical influence over ice spread around the surroundings like invisible threads. This ability drastically increased her versatility in combat.

Elena adapted instantly. She created a portal directly in front of herself and another to her right, diving through to emerge at a different position while the ice shards collided where she'd been standing.

She literally only knew one space weave, but she had mastered it to shocking levels!

'Her ice remains under active control,' Elena noted while circling. 'But maintaining that control across multiple constructs divides her concentration.'

She tested this theory immediately, creating four small portals in rapid succession, each one launching tiny metal darts at insane speeds.

Mirabella had to choose; control her existing ice or defend against the incoming projectiles.

CLANG!

Mirabella chose defense, bringing her ice shards back to deflect the darts. But Elena had already moved, using the distraction to close distance.

She created a portal pair mid-sprint, one entrance at her feet, the exit appearing above Mirabella.

The ice mage looked up to see Elena dropping from above, but this was spatial combat—the expected attack angle.

Mirabella formed a huge ice spike directly above her head, forcing Elena to create another portal mid-fall to redirect herself.

CRACK! BANG!

Elena emerged horizontally from another portal, but Mirabella was ready. She'd formed a needle of ice in the air—thin as paper but sharp as a razor—directly in Elena's trajectory.

Elena's Phoenix Vision caught the temperature differential just in time.

Since she was still midair and hadn't regained her balance from the rapid change in directions, she could only create another portal to pass through the ice needle, but the rapid portal creation was taking its toll.

Each spatial doorway required intense concentration, and she'd already created eight in thirty seconds!

WHOOSH!

Mirabella pressed her advantage with tactical brilliance. She began creating ice needles—hundreds of them, each no larger than a fingernail—suspended in the air around her.

Her Stage 3 rune ability continued to work at full power, the magical enhancement allowing her to maintain control over such a vast number of individual constructs, something that would have been absolutely impossible otherwise.

But instead of launching them as projectiles, she scattered them throughout the arena with deliberate placement.

The needles spread like deadly seeds, hovering at different heights and positions, creating an ominous glittering field that caught the light.

Elena's Phoenix Vision immediately recognized the threat. The needles weren't meant to strike—they were positioned for something else. She began reaching for the equipment pouches at her belt, fingers finding the sets of balanced throwing darts she always carried.

"Detonate," Mirabella said quietly.

CRACK! CRACK! CRACK!

Every ice needle exploded simultaneously into clouds of superfine ice crystals. The detonations weren't violent, they were controlled dispersals that filled the entire arena with a freezing mist.

The air itself became thick with ice particles so small they hung suspended like frozen fog.

Elena felt the cold immediately, the icy mist clinging to her skin and clothes. Her movements began to slow as the numbing cold penetrated her muscles.

The mist was so dense it reduced normal visibility to mere meters.

But Elena's Phoenix Vision wasn't normal sight. While the ice particles interfered with energy perception, she could still see heat signatures—and Mirabella's body temperature, even despite her attempt to reduce it with her ice, stood out like a beacon in the frozen fog.

Phoenix Vision was too powerful for this.

Swoosh!

Mirabella's dagger struck from the mist, expecting to catch Elena blind. But Elena had already sidestepped, her enhanced vision having tracked Mirabella's approach perfectly.

More importantly, she'd identified exactly where Mirabella was standing.

Elena created four portal pairs in rapid succession, each entrance hidden at different points in the mist around her, all exits positioned in a ring around Mirabella's heat signature. She began throwing darts through each portal in sequence.

WHOOSH! CLINK!

Mirabella heard the portals forming but couldn't see them in her own mist. The first dart grazed her shoulder before she even realized the attack pattern.

She dodged left, directly into the path of the second dart from another portal. Her hasty ice shield deflected it, but now she was on pure defense, unable to locate where the attacks originated.

"Clever," Mirabella admitted, spinning to track the whistle of projectiles. "Using my own concealment against me."

Elena didn't respond verbally since that would give away her position. Instead, she relocated silently and created new portal pairs. Her top quality darts were more than enough to deal lethal damage.

Her Phoenix Vision tracked Mirabella's heat signature perfectly while the ice weaver was essentially fighting blind in her own mist. Another volley of darts forced Mirabella to create a defensive wall of ice.

CRACK!

But then Mirabella did something unexpected. She raised both hands and began drawing the mist back toward herself—not dissipating it, but condensing it.

Every particle of ice in the arena began flowing back, streams of frozen crystal converging on her position.

Elena watched through her Phoenix Vision as the ice mist compressed, getting denser and denser. The temperature readings were dropping precipitously at the convergence point. Mirabella was combining all that dispersed ice into something singular.

WHOOSH!

A single ice needle formed in front of Mirabella—but this one was different. It was only a few inches long but impossibly dense, containing all the ice from the mist compressed into one projectile.

It gleamed with an inner light that spoke of dangerous magical concentration. The air around it warped from the extreme cold.

"Every particle of ice from that mist, compressed," Mirabella said, her voice strained from the concentration required. "Let's see you portal this."

Bang!

She launched it with devastating force. The needle moved so fast Elena barely had time to react, and when she created a portal to redirect it, she discovered the problem—the needle was so cold it began crystallizing the portal's edges on contact!

The spatial doorway became unstable, flickering dangerously. And then...

CRACK!

Elena had to abandon the failing portal and dive aside. The needle punched through the arena wall behind her like it was paper, leaving a hole through her shoulder that was rimmed with frost that spread outward in crackling patterns.

But the compression had cost Mirabella. She was breathing heavily, and Elena's Phoenix Vision showed her body temperature fluctuating, signs of mental exhaustion. Creating and controlling that much ice, then compressing it to such density, had drained her reserves significantly.

So Elena had a pierced shoulder that was rapidly freezing over, and Mirabella had a splitting headache and low mental reserves.

It was anyone's game now.
